perturbation.original_text: Independent Contractor shall use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of performing the Services specified in the applicable SOW and not for any other purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Independent Contractor may use the Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever, including purposes unrelated to performing the Services specified in the applicable SOW.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original purpose restriction. The original text restricts the use of Confidential Information to the specified services. The modified text removes any restriction on use, creating a direct in-text contradiction and undermining the agreement's intent to protect confidential information. It can be used for *any* purpose whatsoever.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except to the extent permitted by Section 3 below, Independent Contractor will not disclose the Confidential Information, in whole or in part, to any other party. In fulfilling its obligations under this Agreement, Independent Contractor shall use at least the same standard of care it uses to protect its own information of similar kind, but not less than a reasonable standard of care.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricted the use of Confidential Information to the specified services in the SOW. The changed text removes this restriction, allowing its use for any purpose. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which requires the Independent Contractor to use the 'same standard of care it uses to protect its own information of similar kind, but not less than a reasonable standard of care' and 'not disclose the Confidential Information'. Using the information for *any* purpose is a clear contradiction as it undermines the very premise of confidentiality and restricted use.

perturbation.original_text: Except to the extent permitted by Section 3 below, Independent Contractor will not disclose the Confidential Information, in whole or in part, to any other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Independent Contractor may disclose the Confidential Information, in whole or in part, to any other party, without restriction.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the non-disclosure provision. The original text strictly prohibits disclosure except as permitted in Section 3. The modified text permits disclosure without restriction, creating a clear in-text contradiction that nullifies the confidentiality obligation. There is no longer a requirement to keep the information confidential, can disclose to *any* other party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Independent Contractor acknowledges and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Exhibit B by Independent Contractor and that the Company shall be entitled to specific performance, including without limitation injunctive relief, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for breach of this Exhibit B but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity. Independent Contractor agrees to reimburse the Company for costs and expenses (including without limitation attorneys' fees) incurred by the Company in connection with the enforcement of this Exhibit B.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Independent Contractor to disclose Confidential Information without restriction, directly contradicting the contradicted text which provides remedies for breach of confidentiality. The original text prohibited such disclosure, making a breach possible. Now, the changed text removes this prohibition, effectively eliminating the possibility of a breach and therefore negating the relevance and enforceability of the remedies outlined in the contradicted text. This creates a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In fulfilling its obligations under this Agreement, Independent Contractor shall use at least the same standard of care it uses to protect its own information of similar kind, but not less than a reasonable standard of care.
perturbation.changed_text: Independent Contractor has no obligation to use any standard of care to protect the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change eliminates the standard of care requirement. The original text mandates a reasonable standard of care. The modified text removes this obligation entirely, creating a direct in-text contradiction that negates the protection afforded to Confidential Information. This completely removes the standard of care.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed by the Company to Independent Contractor shall be and shall remain the Company's property. Upon termination of the Agreement, Independent Contractor shall redeliver all tangible Confidential Information furnished by the Company. Except to the extent Independent Contractor is advised in writing by counsel that such action is prohibited by law, Independent Contractor will also destroy all written material, memoranda, notes, and other writings or recordings whatsoever prepared by it based upon, containing, or otherwise reflecting any Confidential Information. Any Confidential Information that is not returned or destroyed, including without limitation any oral Confidential Information, shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Exhibit B.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states "Independent Contractor has no obligation to use any standard of care to protect the Confidential Information." This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which implies an obligation to protect confidential information even after termination of the agreement. Specifically, the contradicted text states "Any Confidential Information that is not returned or destroyed, including without limitation any oral Confidential Information, shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Exhibit B." The changed text removes any standard of care, while the contradicted text assumes a continuing obligation, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination of the Agreement, Independent Contractor shall redeliver all tangible Confidential Information furnished by the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination of the Agreement, Independent Contractor is not required to redeliver any Confidential Information furnished by the Company.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the requirement to return confidential information upon termination. The original clause clearly states the obligation to redeliver. The modified text explicitly removes this obligation, creating a direct conflict. There is no longer the need to return the info.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except to the extent Independent Contractor is advised in writing by counsel that such action is prohibited by law, Independent Contractor will also destroy all written material, memoranda, notes, and other writings or recordings whatsoever prepared by it based upon, containing, or otherwise reflecting any Confidential Information. Any Confidential Information that is not returned or destroyed, including without limitation any oral Confidential Information, shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Exhibit B.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that upon termination, the Independent Contractor 'shall redeliver all tangible Confidential Information.' The changed text states that the Independent Contractor 'is not required to redeliver any Confidential Information.' This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which discusses destroying or returning Confidential Information and states that any Confidential Information that is not returned or destroyed remains subject to confidentiality obligations, thus implying that the Independent Contractor is obligated to return Confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: Independent Contractor acknowledges and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Exhibit B by Independent Contractor and that the Company shall be entitled to specific performance, including without limitation injunctive relief, as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.changed_text: Independent Contractor acknowledges and agrees that money damages shall be the sole and exclusive remedy for any breach of this Exhibit B by Independent Contractor, and the Company shall not be entitled to specific performance or injunctive relief.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the remedies available to the Company in case of a breach. The original clause states that money damages are insufficient and the Company is entitled to specific performance (like injunctive relief). The modified text limits the remedy to money damages alone, removing the possibility of specific performance, leading to an in-text contradiction. This changes the remedy in case of a breach to *money damages only*.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for breach of this Exhibit B but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity. Independent Contractor agrees to reimburse the Company for costs and expenses (including without limitation attorneys' fees) incurred by the Company in connection with the enforcement of this Exhibit B.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that money damages shall be the *sole and exclusive remedy*, while the contradicted text states that such remedy *shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy* but shall be in addition to all other remedies. This is a direct contradiction, as one clause explicitly limits the remedy to money damages, while the other explicitly states that the remedy is not limited to just one type. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
